nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Gifford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Giffard, either from the Middle English personal name Gifard, derived from an Old French name of this form deriving from ancient Germanic geb- ‘give, gift’ + hard- ‘hardy, brave’, or perhaps sometimes a nickname from Old French gif(f)ard ‘chubby-cheeked, bloated’.

variants.name: Jeffords
meanings: English: variant of Gifford , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Hugh de Gifford was born about 1195, in Warminster, Wiltshire, England, his father, Sir Walter Giffard of Boyton, was 45 and his mother, Lady Walter Giffard of Boyton, was 27. He had at least 5 sons and 6 daughters with Sybil de Cormeilles. He died about 1246, in his hometown, at the age of 53.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hugh de Gifford est né environ 1195, à Warminster, Wiltshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Walter Giffard of Boyton, avait 45 ans et sa mère, Lady Walter Giffard of Boyton, avait 27 ans. Il a eu au moins 5 fils et 6 filles avec Sybil de Cormeilles. Il est décédé environ 1246, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 53 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Hugh de Gifford(이)가 대략 1195년 영국 윌츠히르 워민스터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Walter Giffard of Boyton(은)는 45세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Walter Giffard of Boyton(은)는 27세였다. 그는 Sybil de Cormeilles(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1246년 그의 고향에서 53세의 나이로 사망했다.
